findings.claim:
VIP interneurons, themselves regulated by neuromodulators, may therefore enable selective patterns of activity to propagate through the cortex, by generating a "spotlight of attention".

Significance statement

Most inhibitory interneurons have axons restricted to a nearby area and target excitatory neighbors indiscriminately, raising the issue of how neuronal activity can propagate through cortical circuits.

These results establish that cortical inhibition modulates the spatial resolution of visual processing and add further evidence demonstrating that feature selectivity depends, not only on the feedforward excitatory projections into the cortex, but also on dynamic intracortical modulations by specific forms of inhibition.

Significance statement

We demonstrate that interneurons expressing vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP+) play a causal role in regulating the spatial frequency (SF) tuning of neurons in mouse visual cortex.

findings.claim: Similar to what has been shown in the barrel cortex ( Prönneke et al., 2015 ; Almási et al., 2019 ) and visual cortex ( Gonchar et al., 2007 ), we found in the motor cortex that most VIP INs were located in L2/3 ( Supplementary Figure 3 ).
findings.claim_source_sentence: Similar to what has been shown in the barrel cortex ( Prönneke et al., 2015 ; Almási et al., 2019 ) and visual cortex ( Gonchar et al., 2007 ), we found in the motor cortex that most VIP INs were located in L2/3 ( Supplementary Figure 3 ).

findings.claim_source_sentence: Short-term depression at VIP IN→MC synapses was described in the motor cortex ( Figure 1F ) and the auditory and medial prefrontal cortices ( Pi et al., 2013 ), while short-term facilitation was described in the somatosensory cortex ( Walker et al., 2016 ) and visual cortex ( Campagnola et al., 2022 ).

findings.claim_source_sentence: Among various classes of INs, vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing (VIP<sup>+</sup> ) INs preferentially target other INs in several brain regions and thereby directly modulate the GABAergic system.
findings.evidence: Morpho-physiological properties and connectivity of vasoactive intestinal polypeptide-expressing interneurons in the mouse hippocampal dentate gyrus

findings.claim_source_sentence: Abstract Aim Despite dysfunctional vasoactive intestinal polypeptide‐positive interneurons (VIP‐INs) being linked to the emergence of neurodevelopmental disorders, the temporal profile of VIP‐IN functional maturation and cortical network integration remains unclear.

findings.claim: In contrast, as described earlier, a disinhibitory circuit comprised of VIP cells and SST cells plays a key role in control of the gain of visual responses and in facilitation of plasticity by locomotion in adult visual cortex, whereas PV neurons do not show consistent responses to locomotion .
findings.claim_source_sentence: In contrast, as described earlier, a disinhibitory circuit comprised of VIP cells and SST cells plays a key role in control of the gain of visual responses and in facilitation of plasticity by locomotion in adult visual cortex, whereas PV neurons do not show consistent responses to locomotion ( 3 , 4 ).
findings.evidence: Stimulus-specific enhancement in mouse visual cortex requires GABA but not VIP-peptide release from VIP interneurons

findings.claim_source_sentence: A candidate mechanism is the disinhibition of pyramidal cells through vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) and somatostatin (SOM) positive interneurons.
findings.evidence: Attentional modulation is orthogonal to disinhibition by VIP interneurons in primary visual cortex

findings.claim: Tasic et al. (2016) used scRNA-seq to identify 49 transcriptomic cell types in adult mouse primary visual cortex (V1), including 23 GABAergic, 19 glutamatergic, and 7 non-neuronal types.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We classified 1,679 high-quality single cells with extensive characterization into 49 transcriptomic cell types in adult mouse primary visual cortex.
findings.evidence: Single-cell RNA-seq on 1,679 cells from adult mouse V1; clustered into 49 transcriptomic types: 23 GABAergic, 19 glutamatergic, 7 non-neuronal.

findings.claim: Tasic et al. (2016) identified 18 GABAergic transcriptomic cell types belonging to three major classes named after their markers: Vip, Pvalb, and Sst, plus an Ndnf class.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our analysis identifies 18 transcriptomic cell types belonging to three previously described major classes of GABAergic cells named after the corresponding markers Vip (vasoactive intestinal peptide), Pvalb (parvalbumin), and Sst (somatostatin).
findings.evidence: Hierarchical clustering of GABAergic neurons recovered the canonical Vip/Sst/Pvalb classes plus Ndnf, with Vip subdividing into multiple t-types including Vip-Chat, Vip-Gpc3, Vip-Mybpc1, Vip-Sncg, Vip-Parm1.

findings.claim: Tasic et al. (2016) confirmed that the Vip-Chat transcriptomic type uniquely expresses choline acetyltransferase among Vip-positive cells, consistent with previous reports of bipolar VIP/ChAT neurons in upper cortical layers.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In agreement with previous reports, our Vip-Chat transcriptomic type is located in upper cortical layers, and it displays unique expression of choline acetyltransferase (Chat) in Vip-positive cells.
findings.evidence: ISH/scRNA-seq co-localization shows Chat expression in upper-layer Vip-Chat cluster cells; consistent with prior reports of cholinergic VIP bipolar interneurons.
findings.study_system: mouse V1 cortex; SMART-seq scRNA-seq + ISH validation

findings.claim: Tasic et al. (2018) defined six GABAergic subclasses (Sst, Pvalb, Vip, Lamp5, Sncg, Serpinf1) plus distinct Sst-Chodl and Meis2-Adamts19 types, with the major division reflecting MGE versus CGE developmental origin.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We define six subclasses of GABAergic cells: Sst, Pvalb, Vip, Lamp5, Sncg and Serpinf1, and two distinct types: Sst-Chodl and Meis2-Adamts19. The major division among GABAergic types largely corresponds to their developmental origin in the medial ganglionic eminence (Pvalb and Sst subclasses) or caudal ganglionic eminence (Lamp5, Sncg, Serpinfl and Vip subclasses).
findings.evidence: scRNA-seq of 23,822 cells from VISp and ALM yielded a hierarchical taxonomy with these six GABAergic subclasses; CGE-derived (Lamp5/Vip/Sncg/Serpinf1) form interconnected neighborhoods in the constellation diagram.
findings.study_system: mouse VISp + ALM; SMART-seq v4 scRNA-seq

findings.claim: Tasic et al. (2018) reported that within the Lamp5, Vip, Sncg and Serpinf1 subclasses transcriptomic types correspond to neurogliaform, bipolar, single bouquet and cholecystokinin (CCK) basket cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Within the Lamp5, Vip, Sncg and Serpinfl subclasses, we find evidence for neurogliaform, bipolar, single bouquet and cholecystokinin (CCK) basket cell types.
findings.evidence: Cross-referencing marker expression, layer position, and morphology to t-types maps Sncg/Vip-Sncg classes to CCK+ basket cells and bipolar VIP types.

findings.claim: Hodge et al. (2019) identified 75 transcriptomic cell types in human middle temporal gyrus (MTG), with VIP the most diverse interneuron subclass (21 types).
findings.claim_source_sentence: VIP was the most diverse subclass (21 types), with many types enriched in upper layers.
findings.evidence: Single-nucleus RNA-seq of 15,928 nuclei from human MTG (8 donors) yielded 75 t-types; VIP subclass split into 21 types vs ~14 in mouse, indicating expanded VIP diversity in human cortex.

findings.claim: Hodge et al. (2019) found that interneuron-discriminating gene sets were largely conserved between mouse and human, supporting alignment of homologous interneuron subtypes (~37 homologous t-types) despite species-specific expression differences.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Matching cell types requires shared expression patterns between species, and we find that gene families (mean = 21 genes/set) that best discriminate mouse interneurons also discriminate human interneurons.
findings.evidence: MetaNeighbor cross-species mapping aligned ~37 homologous interneuron t-types between human MTG and mouse VISp/ALM; VIP and SST subclasses showed conserved markers but expanded type diversity in human.
findings.study_system: human MTG vs mouse VISp/ALM; cross-species snRNA-seq

findings.claim: Yao et al. (2021) profiled ~1.3 million cells from adult mouse isocortex and hippocampal formation to derive a transcriptomic cell type taxonomy with comprehensive glutamatergic and GABAergic neuron repertoires.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We profiled ~1.3 million cells covering the entire adult mouse isocortex and HPF and derived a transcriptomic cell type taxonomy revealing a comprehensive repertoire of glutamatergic and GABAergic neuron types.
findings.evidence: 10x Genomics + SMART-seq scRNA-seq of ~1.3M cells across isocortex and HPF; hierarchical taxonomy with neighborhoods, subclasses (Lamp5, Sncg, Vip in CGE; Sst Chodl, Sst, Pvalb in MGE), supertypes, and clusters.
findings.study_system: mouse isocortex and HPF; 10x + SMART-seq scRNA-seq

findings.claim: Yao et al. (2021) identified the Vip subclass as comprising six supertypes within the CGE neighborhood of mouse isocortex+HPF, with additional Vip-related supertypes (e.g., Vip Cbln4 HPF) restricted to hippocampal formation.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the CGE neighborhood, the Lamp5 (mostly neurogliaform cells), Sncg and Vip subclasses are divided into 4, 5 (one containing Vip cells), and 6 supertypes, respectively.
findings.evidence: Vip subclass partitions into 6 supertypes (further into ~30 clusters); HPF-specific Vip Cbln4 supertype unique to hippocampus, Qrfpr+ clusters within Vip Cbln4 HPF marked by CA3-related expression.

findings.claim: Yao et al. (2023) reported a whole-brain cell type atlas of adult mouse organized into 34 classes, 338 subclasses, 1,201 supertypes and 5,322 clusters using ~4 million scRNA-seq cells and ~4.3 million MERFISH cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The atlas is hierarchically organized into 4 nested levels of classification: 34 classes, 338 subclasses, 1,201 supertypes and 5,322 clusters.
findings.evidence: Combined scRNA-seq (~4M cells QC) + MERFISH (~4.3M cells) on whole adult mouse brain produced hierarchical taxonomy; Vip GABAergic subclass observed across cortex and other regions, with 5,322 leaf clusters.
findings.study_system: whole adult mouse brain; 10x scRNA-seq + MERFISH

findings.claim: Yao et al. (2023) found Slc18a3 and Chat expression in several clusters within the Vip GABAergic subclass of isocortex, but expression at cluster level did not cross thresholds for labeling them cholinergic.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We also found Slc18a3 and Chat expression in several clusters in the Vip GABA subclass in isocortex, but its expression at cluster level did not cross our threshold to label these clusters as cholinergic.
findings.evidence: Whole-brain cluster-level marker analysis: Vip-Chat clusters express Chat/Slc18a3 at sub-threshold levels, supporting bipolar VIP-ChAT identity reported in primary visual cortex but with low Chat expression.

findings.claim: BICCN (2021) integrated snRNA-seq datasets across mouse, marmoset and human primary motor cortex (MOp/M1) to identify 45 conserved t-types including 24 GABAergic types within a consensus cross-species taxonomy.
findings.claim_source_sentence: To establish a consensus classification of MOp and M1 cell types among mouse, human and marmoset, we integrated snRNA-seq datasets across species and identified 45 conserved t-types, including 24 GABAergic.
findings.evidence: Cross-species snRNA-seq integration of MOp/M1 yielded 45 conserved t-types; CGE-derived subclasses (Lamp5, Sncg, Vip) preserved across all three species; human cell-type taxonomy 127 t-types, marmoset 94 t-types.
findings.study_system: mouse + marmoset + human MOp/M1; snRNA-seq + MERFISH + epigenomic

findings.claim: Bakken et al. (2021) showed that more Vip subtype consensus clusters could be resolved by pairwise alignment between human and marmoset M1 than between either primate and mouse, indicating closer homology of VIP types within primates.
findings.claim_source_sentence: More consensus clusters could be resolved by pairwise alignment between humans and marmosets than between either of these primates and mice, particularly for Vip subtypes.
findings.evidence: Cross-species integrative clustering of MOp/M1 across human, marmoset, mouse: Vip_2 consensus type splits into multiple homologous primate types, indicating greater intra-primate VIP diversity than primate-rodent.
findings.study_system: human + marmoset + mouse M1/MOp; snRNA-seq + snmC-seq2 + snATAC-seq

findings.claim: Zhang et al. (2021) used MERFISH to generate a molecularly defined and spatially resolved cell atlas of mouse primary motor cortex (MOp), targeting 258 genes including canonical interneuron markers (Vip, Sst, Pvalb, Lamp5).
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we used a single-cell transcriptome-imaging method, multiplexed error-robust fluorescence in situ hybridization (MERFISH), to generate a molecularly defined and spatially resolved cell atlas of the mouse primary motor cortex.
findings.evidence: MERFISH on 258-gene panel; identified Vip, Sncg, Lamp5 CGE subclasses preferentially in upper cortical layers, Sst/Pvalb in deeper; cluster correspondence with scRNA-seq/snRNA-seq.

findings.claim: Liu et al. (2021) generated a single-cell DNA methylation atlas of mouse brain identifying CGE-derived Lamp5 and CGE-Vip lineages distinguishable by Bcl11b expression among other markers.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Bcl11b distinguishes OLF-Exc and IT neurons in the excitatory lineage and distinguishes CGE-Lamp5 and CGE-Vip in the inhibitory lineage.
findings.evidence: snmC-seq2 single-cell methylome atlas of 110,294 mouse brain cells; Bcl11b methylation pattern separates CGE-Lamp5 from CGE-Vip lineages, supporting transcriptomic taxonomy at the epigenomic level.
findings.study_system: mouse whole brain; snmC-seq2 single-cell methylome

findings.claim: Gouwens et al. (2020) defined 28 morphoelectric-transcriptomic (MET) types of cortical GABAergic interneurons through Patch-seq integration, providing a unified framework that reconciles ~60 t-types with ~26 me-types.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Through multimodal integrated analysis we define 28 met-types that have congruent morphological, electrophysiological, and transcriptomic properties and robust mutual predictability.
findings.evidence: Patch-seq on 4,200+ cortical GABAergic neurons (mouse VISp); integrative classification yielded 28 MET-types; Vip subclass split into multiple MET-types defined by congruent morphology, electrophysiology, and t-type.

findings.claim: Gouwens et al. (2020) showed that GABAergic interneurons in electrophysiology UMAP space largely segregated into 4 clouds corresponding to Lamp5, Vip, Sst, and Pvalb transcriptomic subclasses, supporting subclass-level concordance between transcriptomic and electrophysiological identity.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Examining the location of the 2,955 cells with 'highly consistent' t-type mapping demonstrated that, at the subclass level, most GABAergic interneurons occupied one of 4 large clouds in the electrophysiology UMAP projection, corresponding to the Lamp5, Vip, Sst, and Pvalb transcriptomic subclasses.
findings.evidence: Of 4,270 Patch-seq cells, 2,955 with 'highly consistent' t-type mapping clustered into Lamp5/Vip/Sst/Pvalb electrophysiological clouds; subclass identity is the most reliable correspondence between modalities.

findings.claim: Jorstad et al. (2023) reported a human cortex GABAergic taxonomy of 45 transcriptomic types across four canonical interneuron subclasses (PVALB, SST, VIP, LAMP5/PAX6) using Patch-seq with enhancer-AAV labeling for VIP enrichment.
findings.claim_source_sentence: This taxonomy contains 45 GABAergic neuronal transcriptomic types across four canonical interneuron subclasses (PVALB, SST, VIP, and LAMP5/PAX6).
findings.evidence: Human cortex Patch-seq with enhancer-AAV (CN2039) labeling enriched VIP recovery; VIP subclass shows diverse types with small soma + bipolar dendrites in L2/3; subclass-level morphoelectric separation confirmed.

findings.claim: Jorstad et al. (2023) identified human-specific L1 interneuron transcriptomic types (e.g., VIP PCDH20 and SST BAGE2) that are homologous to deeper mouse t-types but exhibit no morphologically equivalent neurons in mouse L1, indicating type-specific divergence across species.
findings.claim_source_sentence: No mouse L1 neurons had morphologies resembling the unmatched human L1 t-types (VIP PCDH20 and SST BAGE12, homologous to deeper mouse t-types), and the MC4R subclass was less morphologically distinct, providing evidence for type-specific divergence across species.
findings.evidence: Cross-species Patch-seq comparison of L1 cortical interneurons; human VIP PCDH20 and SST BAGE2 t-types absent from mouse L1; L1 VIP TSPAN12 cells share descending axon collaterals + stellate dendrites + high sag conserved across species.
findings.study_system: human and mouse L1 cortex; Patch-seq + cross-species transcriptomic alignment

findings.claim: Tremblay, Lee, & Rudy (2016) reviewed that the 5HT3aR interneuron group is divided into two subgroups based on VIP expression, with VIP+ neurons constituting ~40% of 5HT3aR INs and being entirely absent from PV or Sst populations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The 5HT3aR group can be divided in two subgroups based on whether or not a 5HT3aR IN expresses vasointestinal pepetide (Vip), which is found in about 40 % of all 5HT3aR INs and is not expressed in PV or Sst neurons (Lee et al., 2010).
findings.evidence: Review consolidating immunohistochemical and Cre-line evidence: VIP neurons account for ~40% of 5HT3aR+ interneurons; PV/SST/VIP segregation is mutually exclusive at the major-class level.

findings.claim: Tremblay et al. (2016) reviewed evidence that VIP+ cortical interneurons co-express subsidiary markers in distinct subpopulations: CCK in multipolar VIP cells, calretinin (CR) in irregular-spiking bipolar VIP/CR+ cells, and choline acetyltransferase (ChAT) in a small VIP/ChAT bipolar subgroup.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Multipolar VIP INs include VIP INs expressing CCK and a group of L6 multipolar VIP cells with an intralaminar axon spanning laterally. Some Vip INs also express the ACh synthetizing enzyme choline acetyl transferase (ChAT). Vip CR+ INs behave differently than Vip CR- neurons in terms of the role of activity in their migration and maturation.
findings.evidence: VIP IN subpopulations: VIP/CCK multipolar (basket-like), VIP/CR bipolar (irregular spiking), VIP/ChAT (cholinergic, upper layer). Intersectional Vip-flp × CR-cre genetics confirmed VIP/CR cells enriched in irregular-spiking bipolar phenotype (He et al. 2016).

findings.claim: Rudy, Fishell, Lee, & Hjerling-Leffler (2011) established that PV-, SST-, and 5HT3aR-expressing neurons together account for nearly 100% of neocortical GABAergic interneurons, with 5HT3aR group containing all VIP+ neurons (~40%) and a non-VIP neurogliaform subgroup.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The 5HT3aR group, which also accounts for ~30% of the total interneuronal population, is heterogeneous and includes all of the neurons that express the neuropeptide VIP, as well as an equally numerous subgroup of neurons that do not express VIP and includes neurogliaform cells.
findings.evidence: Triple-marker (PV/SST/5HT3aR) cytometric scheme accounts for ~100% mouse S1 GABAergic interneurons; 5HT3aR group ~30% of total INs; ~40% of 5HT3aR group express VIP; PV/SST/VIP non-overlapping major classes.

findings.claim: Rudy et al. (2011) reviewed that VIP+ neocortical interneurons comprise multiple subtypes with bipolar, bitufted, or multipolar morphologies expressing different secondary markers (e.g., CR, CCK) with distinct intrinsic electrophysiology.
findings.claim_source_sentence: There seem to be several types of VIP interneurons, including neurons with bipolar, bitufted, and multipolar morphologies expressing different markers and exhibiting different intrinsic electrophysiological properties.
findings.evidence: Within VIP class: bipolar VIP/CR (irregular spiking), bipolar VIP/ChAT, multipolar VIP/CCK (basket-like); intrinsic properties vary across morphological subtypes.

findings.claim: Paul et al. (2017) demonstrated that cardinal GABAergic neuron types in mouse cortex are delineated by a transcriptional architecture encoding their synaptic communication patterns, with VIP class subdivided into interneuron-selective disinhibitory (VIP/CR) and CCK basket subpopulations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We discovered that cardinal GABAergic neuron types are delineated by a transcriptional architecture that encodes their synaptic communication patterns. The HTR3a group includes the Vassoactive intestinal peptide (VIP) and Reelin populations, and the VIP population comprises interneuron-selective dis-inhibitory cells (ISC), Cholecystokinin (CCK) small basket cells (CCKC) and likely additional...
findings.evidence: Bulk RNA-seq of FACS-sorted Cre-line populations representing 6 cardinal types (PCPs); VIP/CR ISCs and VIP/CCK CCKCs identified as distinct populations within VIP; transcriptional features encoding synaptic input/output gene families distinguish each type.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex; bulk RNA-seq of FACS-sorted populations

findings.claim: Mi et al. (2018) demonstrated that early postmitotic interneuron precursors from MGE and CGE already exhibit distinct transcriptional signatures that map onto cardinal mature types (Pvalb, Sst, Vip, Id2, Th, Nos1, Igfbp6), with Vip bipolar vs multipolar segregation evident before circuit integration.
findings.claim_source_sentence: These could be allocated into non-overlapping cardinal types of cortical interneurons (Pvalb, Sst, Vip, Id2, Th, Nos1, Igfbp6). [Including] clear segregation between Sst Martinotti versus non-Martinotti, Vip bipolar versus multipolar, and Id2 neurogliaform versus non-neurogliaform interneuron subtypes.
findings.evidence: Drop-seq scRNA-seq of E13.5 MGE and E14.5 CGE/LGE precursors revealed early diversification into mature interneuron classes; Vip class trajectory bifurcates into bipolar and multipolar transcriptomic signatures before cortical entry.

findings.claim: Zeisel et al. (2018) profiled the mouse nervous system at single-cell resolution and discovered cell-type organization is governed by three overlapping principles: major class, developmental origin, and neurotransmitter type, with cortical Vip/Sst/Pvalb interneurons defining a major branch of GABAergic CGE/MGE-derived telencephalic interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The resulting arrangement of cell types revealed that the mammalian nervous system is organized according to three overlapping and interacting principles: major class (e.g., neurons, astrocytes), developmental origin (e.g., telencephalon, diencephalon, midbrain, hindbrain) and neurotransmitter type.
findings.evidence: scRNA-seq of 509,876 cells across mouse nervous system; hierarchical taxonomy with cortical interneurons forming distinct subclasses by marker (Vip, Sst, Pvalb) within developmental origin, validating cardinal interneuron classification at whole-nervous-system scale.

findings.claim: Cadwell et al. (2016) introduced Patch-seq, a method combining whole-cell patch-clamp electrophysiology, single-cell RNA-seq, and morphological characterization on the same neuron, enabling multimodal classification of neuronal cell types in mouse cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: To improve the classification of neuronal cell types and the functional characterization of single neurons, we present Patch-seq, a method that combines whole-cell electrophysiological patch-clamp recordings, single-cell RNA-sequencing and morphological characterization.
findings.evidence: Demonstration on 58 mouse cortical interneurons; transcriptome quality after pipette aspiration sufficient for clustering; correlation of ephys features with transcriptomic identity established workflow used in subsequent VIP MET-type studies.

findings.claim: Fuzik et al. (2016) developed an independent Patch-seq variant combining whole-cell patch-clamp recordings with full-transcriptome single-cell RNA-seq from neocortical pyramidal cells and interneurons, establishing a method now widely used for VIP morphoelectric-transcriptomic typing.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we present a method to obtain full transcriptome data from single neocortical pyramidal cells and interneurons after whole-cell patch-clamp recordings in mouse brain slices.
findings.evidence: Patch-seq workflow: patch-clamp ephys → aspirate cytoplasm → STRT-seq RNA-seq → cluster cells transcriptomically; published concurrently with Cadwell et al. 2016 establishing two independent multimodal workflows.
findings.study_system: mouse neocortex slices; Patch-seq method paper

findings.claim: Berg et al. (2021) used Patch-seq on human neurosurgical cortical tissue to demonstrate that the transcriptomically-defined L5 extratelencephalic (ET) projection-neuron type has conserved morpho-electric properties between human and rodent, validating Patch-seq cross-species typing in the same paradigm used for VIP MET-types.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Patch-seq recordings, where transcriptome, physiology and morphology were assayed from the same cell, revealed many conserved morpho-electric properties of human and rodent L5 ET neurons. Divergent properties were often subtler than differences between L5 cell types within these two species.
findings.evidence: Patch-seq on human L5 cortex from neurosurgical specimens; cross-species comparison shows conserved electrophysiology of L5 ET pyramidal neurons; same workflow applies to inhibitory cell typing.

findings.claim: Agmon & Barth (2024) reviewed that the number of proposed cortical SST interneuron transcriptomic types has been rising exponentially, doubling or tripling between studies, and argued that the ~10 'supertypes' level (12 SST supertypes among 30 GABAergic supertypes in Yao 2021) most closely matches morphology/electrophysiology/neurochemistry-defined classes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The number of proposed transcriptomic types ('t-types' or 'clusters') of cortical SST (as well as other) interneurons has been rising exponentially, doubling or tripling from one study to the next.
findings.evidence: Quantitative review: out of 30 GABAergic supertypes in Yao et al. 2021 mouse isocortex/HPF taxonomy, 12 are SST; supertypes correspond more cleanly than t-types to traditional morpho-electric classes; provides a critical perspective on lumping/splitting in CGE-derived (incl. VIP) typing.

findings.claim: Bakken et al. (2021) found that primate dLGN exhibits expanded GABAergic interneuron diversity relative to mouse, suggesting similar species-specific elaboration of inhibitory diversity that may parallel expanded VIP type diversity in primate cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Alignment of transcriptome profiles across species highlights expanded diversity of GABAergic neurons in primate versus mouse dLGN and homologous types of TC neurons in primates that are distinct from TC neurons in mouse.
findings.evidence: Cross-species snRNA-seq of human, macaque, mouse dLGN; expanded primate GABAergic types parallels VIP/SST expansion seen in primate cortex.

findings.claim: Miller et al. (2020) introduced the Common Cell Type Nomenclature (CCN) framework for tracking cortical cell types across studies, using Pvalb/Sst (MGE-derived) and Vip/Sncg/Lamp5 (CGE-derived) canonical interneuron classes as the reference taxonomy backbone.
findings.claim_source_sentence: For GABAergic interneurons, developmental origin may define cell types by their canonical marker gene profile established early in development, with Pvalb and Sst labeling cell types derived from the medial ganglionic eminence and Vip, Sncg, and Lamp5 labeling cell types derived from the caudal ganglionic eminence.
findings.evidence: CCN assigns unique taxonomy IDs (CCN[YYYYMMDD][#]) and persistent accession ids to cell types; explicitly anchored on Vip/Sncg/Lamp5 CGE subclasses; enables cross-study mapping of VIP types and serves as standard backbone for BICCN and Allen Institute taxonomies.

findings.claim: Schubert et al. (2024) summarized that mammalian cortex contains ~60 inhibitory transcriptomic types organized into PV, SST, VIP, and Lamp5/Sncg subclasses, with VIP cells primarily targeting other GABAergic subclasses (notably SST) to mediate disinhibition of local pyramidal neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Inhibitory neurons were subdivided into parvalbumin (PV), somatostatin (SST), vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP), and 'other (i.e. Lamp5/Sncg)' subpopulations, which altogether comprise ~60 t-types. The majority of VIP interneurons preferentially target other GABAergic subclasses, notably SST cells, consequently disinhibiting local pyramidal neurons.
findings.evidence: Multimodal review consolidates current consensus: mouse cortex ~60 inhibitory t-types within 4 subclasses; VIP→SST→PYR disinhibitory motif is canonical; Pvalb-Vipr2 chandelier subtype noted within VIP nomenclature.

findings.claim: Boldog et al. (2018) identified a human-specific cortical interneuron type ('rosehip cell') in human L1 not matching any mouse Vip/Sst/Pvalb cluster, while several other human L1 t-types (i6, i8, i9, i10, i11) matched mouse Vip+ types, supporting both conserved and human-specific elaboration of CGE-derived interneurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Many rosehip marker genes are not expressed in Smad3 or other Pvalb/Sst/Vip-negative mouse cell types or the complete set of mouse GABAergic types. Clusters i6, i8, i9, i11, and i10 matched mouse Vip+ clusters, and cluster i2 matched mouse Sst+ clusters.
findings.evidence: Patch-seq + snRNA-seq of human L1 cortex; rosehip type lacks mouse homolog; multiple VIP+ L1 clusters share marker signatures with mouse Vip+ types — confirming both conserved CGE-derived VIP types and human-specific innovations.

findings.claim: Scala et al. (2021) used Patch-seq on >1,300 mouse motor cortex (M1) neurons to show that broad transcriptomic families (Vip, Pvalb, Sst) had distinct non-overlapping morpho-electric phenotypes, but individual t-types within a family were not well separated in morpho-electric space, indicating continuous phenotypic variation within VIP and other subclasses.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that, although broad families of transcriptomic types (those expressing Vip, Pvalb, Sst and so on) had distinct and essentially non-overlapping morpho-electric phenotypes, individual transcriptomic types within the same family were not well separated in the morpho-electric space.
findings.evidence: Patch-seq on >1,300 cells from adult mouse M1; subclass-level morphoelectric separation robust; t-type-level separation continuous (e.g., Vip subtypes form a continuum rather than discrete clusters), challenging strict t-type/MET-type 1:1 correspondence below the subclass level.

findings.claim: Scala et al. (2021) identified a transcriptomically isolated Vip Lamp5 Lhx6 t-type that corresponds morphologically to deep L5/L6 neurogliaform cells (NGCs), an exception to the typical upper-layer Vip distribution.
findings.claim_source_sentence: One interesting case was the transcriptomically isolated Vip Lamp5 Lhx6 type, which consists of deep L5/L6 neurogliaform cells (NGCs).
findings.evidence: Patch-seq mapping: Vip Lamp5 Lhx6 t-type localizes to deep cortical layers and morphology matches NGC; conflicts with simple 'Vip = upper layer bipolar' rule.

findings.claim: Yao et al. (2021) (BICCN epigenomic atlas) integrated seven scRNA-seq and snRNA-seq datasets of mouse primary motor cortex (MOp) to establish a consensus transcriptomic reference atlas of 116 cell types, including replicable Vip subclasses across datasets and methods.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Computational data integration jointly cluster and identify 116 cell types using all the datasets... resulting reference atlas — containing over 56 neuronal cell types that are highly replicable across analysis methods, sequencing technologies and modalities — is a comprehensive molecular and genomic account.
findings.evidence: Joint clustering of 7 scRNA-seq/snRNA-seq datasets + epigenomic data on >500,000 mouse MOp cells; >56 replicable neuronal types including Vip subclasses; provides epigenomic regulatory elements concordant with transcriptomic VIP types.
findings.study_system: mouse MOp; integrated scRNA-seq + snRNA-seq + snmC-seq + snATAC-seq

findings.claim: Lake et al. (2018) used integrative snDrop-seq + scTHS-seq on >60,000 human brain cells to spatially resolve a VIP+/CALB2+/TAC3+ upper-layer interneuron subpopulation (In1d) corresponding to the canonical VIP/CR class in mouse, validating cross-modal identification of human VIP types.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We were further able to resolve spatially distinct inhibitory neuron subpopulations, including: layer 1 RELN+CCK+CNR1+ In1a; upper layer VIP+CALB2+TAC3+ In1d; PVALB+CA8+ In6a concentrated around layer 4, as well as the more peripheral PVALB+TAC1+ In6b; and two distinct SST positive...
findings.evidence: snDrop-seq + scTHS-seq integration of >60,000 human visual cortex, frontal cortex, cerebellum nuclei; In1d type marked by VIP+CALB2+TAC3+ in upper layers — homologous to mouse Vip-CR class; chromatin accessibility maps regulatory elements driving VIP identity.
findings.study_system: human visual + frontal cortex + cerebellum; snDrop-seq + scTHS-seq

findings.claim: Luo et al. (2017) used single-cell DNA methylation sequencing (snmC-seq) on >6,000 mouse and human frontal cortex nuclei to identify homologous VIP, PV, and SST inhibitory neuron subtypes between species, with methylation patterns identifying VIP-class super-enhancers around Prox1 (a CGE master regulator).
findings.claim_source_sentence: Multiple human neuron clusters showed homology to mouse L5a excitatory neurons (mL5-1), L6a pyramidal neuron (mL6-2), or to VIP, PV, and SST inhibitory neurons. Super-enhancers overlap with key regulatory genes in the associated cell type, such as Prox1 in VIP+ and NDNF+ neurons.
findings.evidence: snmC-seq on >6,000 single nuclei (3,377 mouse + 2,805 human frontal cortex); identified 16 mouse and 21 human neuronal subpopulations; cross-species mapping aligned VIP, PV, SST homologous types; CGE-derived (VIP, NDNF) types share Prox1 super-enhancer.
findings.study_system: mouse and human frontal cortex; snmC-seq DNA methylation

findings.claim: Hrvatin et al. (2019) developed PESCA, a scalable platform combining ATAC-seq with viral barcoding to identify cell-type-specific enhancers, generating viral reagents that target SST cortical interneurons and providing transferable methodology for VIP and PV subtype-specific genetic access.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We validated the efficacy of PESCA in the murine primary visual cortex by identifying GREs that confine AAV expression to somatostatin (SST)-expressing interneurons and showed that these vectors can be used to modulate neuronal activity selectively in SST neurons. These datasets can be used as a resource to identify putative gene regulatory elements as candidates for driving cell-type-specific gene expression for the numerous subtypes of SST, PV or VIP-expressing intraneurons across diverse cortical regions.
findings.evidence: ATAC-seq on FACS-purified PV, SST, VIP cortical nuclei → 323,369 putative GREs → 287 SST-specific candidates screened via AAV in mouse V1; produces enhancer-AAV toolkit applicable to VIP types as used in subsequent Patch-seq studies (Jorstad 2023).
findings.study_system: mouse V1 cortex; ATAC-seq + AAV GRE screening

findings.claim: Gouwens et al. (2022) generated biophysical single-neuron models from cortical Patch-seq data, showing that conductance vectors cluster by transcriptomic subclass (Vip, Sst, Pvalb, Pyr) and that ion-channel composition itself encodes interneuron identity at the subclass level.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We hypothesized that the existence of multiple conductance vectors for a single cell can inform on cell-type representation. The all-active models conductance vectors reflect distinct compositions of cell subclasses that cluster accordingly between major cortical populations. Pvalb cells are adjacent to Sst, while Vip are on the other side.
findings.evidence: Biophysical model fitting on Patch-seq cells; HOF (hall-of-fame) conductance vectors cluster by Vip/Sst/Pvalb/Pyr subclass — demonstrates electrophysiological identity of VIP cells encodes a distinct ion channel program.
findings.study_system: mouse V1 + ALM; Patch-seq + biophysical modeling

findings.claim: Yuste et al. (2020) proposed a community-based transcriptomic classification and nomenclature for neocortical cell types based on a probabilistic cell-type definition, with statistical clusters often corresponding to cell types previously defined by morphology or physiology and appearing conserved across cortical areas and species.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Statistical analyses of these data reveal clusters that often correspond to cell types previously defined by morphological or physiological criteria and that appear conserved across cortical areas and species. To capitalize on these new methods, we propose the adoption of a transcriptome-based taxonomy of cell types for mammalian neocortex.
findings.evidence: Community framework paper; argues for probabilistic transcriptome-based cell-type definition; foundation for cross-study VIP type harmonization (e.g., CCN, BICCN consortium standards).

findings.claim: Granger et al. (2020) demonstrated that nearly all cortical ChAT+ neurons in mice are specialized VIP+ interneurons that co-release GABA strongly onto inhibitory interneurons and acetylcholine sparsely onto layer 1 interneurons, providing functional evidence that VIP-ChAT cells form a transcriptomically and functionally distinct VIP subtype.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show that the nearly all cortical ChAT+ neurons in mice are specialized VIP+ interneurons that release GABA strongly onto other inhibitory interneurons and acetylcholine sparsely onto layer 1 interneurons and other VIP+/ChAT+ interneurons. This differential transmission of ACh and GABA based on the postsynaptic target neuron is reflected in VIP+/ChAT+ interneuron pre-synaptic terminals, as quantitative molecular analysis shows that only a subset of these are specialized to release acetylcholine.
findings.evidence: Whole-cell electrophysiology + optogenetics in ChAT-Cre mice; VIP+/ChAT+ neurons co-release GABA and ACh in target-specific manner; only a subset of presynaptic terminals contain cholinergic machinery; distinct from rare non-VIP ChAT+ cells in mPFC with separate developmental origin.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex (V1, S1, mPFC); ChAT-Cre + Patch-seq + electrophysiology + optogenetics

findings.claim: Smith et al. (2019) revealed that single-cell transcriptomes from mouse cortical neurons encode dense, type-specific intracortical neuropeptide signaling networks: every cortical neuron type expresses multiple NPP and NP-GPCR genes, with VIP, SST, NPY, CCK serving as canonical interneuron-class markers consistent with a long history of immunohistochemical typing.
findings.claim_source_sentence: It is well established that certain neuropeptides, including vasoactive intestinal peptide (VIP), somatostatin (SST), neuropeptide Y (NPY), substance P, and cholecystokinin (CCK), are detectible at high levels in particular subsets of GABAergic cortical neurons. These heat maps also show that every neuron type expresses multiple NPP and NP-GPCR genes and that each of the NPP and NP-GPCR genes is expressed in multiple neuron types.
findings.evidence: Analysis of Tasic 2018 and Zeisel 2018 scRNA-seq data: 18 NPP genes including Vip and 29 NP-GPCR genes show type-specific expression; VIP/CCK/SST/NPY anchor classical immunohistochemical interneuron classification within transcriptomic taxonomy.

findings.claim: Crow et al. (2018) introduced MetaNeighbor, a quantitative framework for assessing cell-type replicability across scRNA-seq datasets, demonstrating high reproducibility of major cortical interneuron classes (Vip, Sst, Pvalb) but progressively lower reproducibility of fine-grained t-types across studies.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we demonstrate our replication framework, MetaNeighbor, that quantifies the degree to which cell types replicate across datasets, and enables rapid identification of clusters with high similarity. Across tasks we find that large sets of variably expressed genes can identify replicable cell types with high accuracy.
findings.evidence: Cross-comparison of 8 scRNA-seq datasets including Tasic 2016, Zeisel 2015, Habib 2017; cortical Vip subclass replicates near-perfectly across studies; finer Vip subtype replicability is dataset-dependent.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex; meta-analysis of 8 scRNA-seq datasets (MetaNeighbor)

findings.claim: De Marco García, Karayannis, & Fishell (2011) showed that activity-dependent migration and dendritic/axonal development before P3 is required for Reelin+ and Calretinin+ CGE-derived cortical interneurons but not for VIP+ interneurons, indicating that VIP+ subtypes follow a distinct developmental program from VIP/CR+ irregular-spiking bipolar cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here we demonstrate that for CGE-derived Re+ and Cr+ (but not VIP+) interneurons, activity is essential before postnatal day 3 (P3) for correct migration, and that after P3, glutamate-mediated activity controls the development of their axons and dendrites.
findings.evidence: Cre-driver labeling + electroporation of CGE: Cr+ and Re+ interneurons require activity for migration & axonal development; VIP+ axons unaltered after manipulation; suggests early divergence of VIP/CR+ vs pure VIP+ subtypes.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex; Cre-driver lineage tracing + electroporation

findings.claim: Ouellet & de Villers-Sidani (2014) tracked CR, VIP, and ChAT marker populations in rat primary auditory cortex (A1) across postnatal development to old age (P9–P750), finding CR, VIP, and ChAT among the most prominent early interneuron markers; CR counts relative to total interneurons were maintained despite absolute count decrease with aging.
findings.claim_source_sentence: At P9, CR, VIP, and ChAT appeared to be the most prominent cortical interneuron markers. CR counts relative to the entire interneuron population were maintained despite the decrease in their absolute count in A1.
findings.evidence: Stereological counts in rat A1 across 5 ages (P9–P750); CR, VIP, ChAT prominent early; relative CR fraction stable across lifespan; provides developmental anchor for VIP/CR/ChAT co-expression timing.
findings.study_system: rat primary auditory cortex (A1); immunohistochemistry across P9–P750

findings.claim: Tosches et al. (2018) inferred from comparative scRNA-seq of reptile pallia that mammalian neocortical glutamatergic layers represent new cell types built by diversifying ancestral gene-regulatory programs, while diverse cortical interneuron classes already existed in the common amniote ancestor.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The transcriptomic signature of glutamatergic neurons in reptilian cortex suggests that mammalian neocortical layers are made of new cell types generated by diversification of ancestral gene-regulatory programs.
findings.evidence: scRNA-seq pallium atlas of two reptilian species, compared to mammalian cortical neuron transcriptomes; argues GABAergic class diversity is ancestral.

findings.claim: A snRNA-seq + spatial transcriptomic atlas of 143 macaque cortical regions resolved 264 transcriptome-defined cell types and showed that the regional distribution of cell types relates to the cortical area's hierarchical position in information processing.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using large-scale single-nucleus RNA sequencing and spatial transcriptomic analysis of 143 macaque cortical regions, we obtained a comprehensive atlas of 264 transcriptome-defined cortical cell types and mapped their spatial distribution across the entire cortex.
findings.evidence: Whole-macaque-cortex snRNA-seq + spatial transcriptomics; 264 transcriptome cell types.

findings.claim: scRNA-seq of 133,454 macaque visual cortex cells distinguished 25 excitatory and 37 inhibitory neuron types and showed that, in cross-species comparison, glutamatergic neurons may be more diverse across species than GABAergic neurons or non-neuronal cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The comparisons also revealed that glutamatergic neurons may be more diverse across species than GABAergic neurons and non-neuronal cells.
findings.evidence: scRNA-seq of macaque V1 (n=133,454 cells); 25 excitatory + 37 inhibitory types; cross-species comparison versus human and mouse.

findings.claim: A snRNA-seq atlas of ~500,000 nuclei from 19 marmoset CNS tissue types mapped 87 subclusters onto a 3D MRI atlas and revealed strong spatial segregation of microglia, OPCs, and astrocytes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Here, we analyze this resource and find strong spatial segregation of microglia, oligodendrocyte progenitor cells, and astrocytes.
findings.evidence: snRNA-seq of ~500,000 nuclei from 19 marmoset CNS regions; 87 subclusters; 3D MRI mapping.
findings.study_system: marmoset CNS snRNA-seq with spatial mapping

findings.claim: Zeisel et al. (2015) applied large-scale scRNA-seq to mouse somatosensory cortex and CA1 and identified 47 molecularly distinct cell subclasses spanning all known major cortical cell types, providing one of the first transcriptomic taxonomies of cortical neurons.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found 47 molecularly distinct subclasses, comprising all known major cell types in the cortex.
findings.evidence: scRNA-seq of mouse somatosensory cortex + hippocampal CA1; 47 molecularly distinct cell subclasses identified.

findings.claim: Rosenberg et al. (2018) introduced SPLiT-seq, a combinatorial-barcoding scRNA-seq method, and applied it to 156,049 nuclei from postnatal mouse brains and spinal cords, identifying more than 100 cell types whose expression patterns reflect function, regional specificity, and differentiation stage.
findings.claim_source_sentence: More than 100 cell types were identified, with gene expression patterns corresponding to cellular function, regional specificity, and stage of differentiation.
findings.evidence: SPLiT-seq snRNA-seq of 156,049 nuclei from P2 and P11 mouse brain and spinal cord.

findings.claim: Matched single-nucleus and single-cell RNA-seq from mouse visual cortex showed that, despite ~11,000 genes detected per whole cell vs ~7,000 per nucleus, closely related neuronal types can be similarly discriminated by snRNA-seq when intronic reads are retained.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Although more transcripts are detected in individual whole cells (~11,000 genes) than nuclei (~7,000 genes), we demonstrate that closely related neuronal cell types can be similarly discriminated with both methods if intronic sequences are included in snRNA-seq analysis.
findings.evidence: Matched snRNA-seq and scRNA-seq from mouse V1; comparison of cell-type discrimination power and per-cell gene detection.

findings.claim: Single-cell transcriptomics of developing Htr3a-GFP+ interneurons distinguished three main molecular types, with MEIS2-expressing interstitial cells originating from the embryonic pallial-subpallial boundary, demonstrating that transcriptional and anatomical parcellation of CGE-derived interneurons is developmentally coupled.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We identify three main molecular types of Htr3a-GFP+ interneurons, each displaying distinct developmental dynamics of gene expression.
findings.evidence: scRNA-seq of Htr3a-GFP+ cortical interneurons during early circuit assembly; identifies three molecular types and MEIS2 as a marker of interstitial subclass.

findings.claim: By bridging Patch-seq morphology to electron-microscopy reconstructions, Sst-positive Martinotti cells were classified into distinct Sst MET-types with different axon myelination and synaptic output connectivity, showing that transcriptomic MET-types correspond to connectivity-distinguishable subgroups.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We further analysed Martinotti cells-a somatostatin (Sst)-positive<sup>17</sup> morphological cell type<sup>18,19</sup>-which were classified successfully into Sst MET-types with distinct axon myelination and synaptic output connectivity patterns.
findings.evidence: Cross-modal linkage of Patch-seq MET-types to large-scale EM connectomics in mouse V1, focusing on SST Martinotti subgroups.
findings.study_system: mouse V1 Patch-seq + large-scale EM connectomics

findings.claim: A re-analysis of five Patch-seq datasets found increased off-target cell-type mRNA contamination in cells from acute brain slices and large variability in mRNA yield, prompting marker-gene-based quality scoring whose application improves the correspondence between transcriptome and electrophysiology.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found an increased likelihood of off-target cell-type mRNA contamination in patch-seq cells from acute brain slices, likely due to the passage of the patch-pipette through the processes of adjacent cells.
findings.evidence: Re-analysis of 5 Patch-seq datasets (mouse brain slices + human iPSC neurons); contamination scoring framework.
findings.study_system: Patch-seq quality reanalysis (mouse cortex / human iPSC)

findings.claim: In mouse barrel cortex, VIP-expressing interneurons constitute about 13% of all GABAergic neurons, with ~60% residing in layers II/III, and layer II/III VIP cells differ significantly from layer IV–VI VIP cells in morphology and membrane properties — implying layer-dependent functional heterogeneity within the VIP class.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In the barrel cortex, approximately 13% of all GABAergic neurons are VIP expressing.
findings.evidence: Quantitative IHC + Vip-FISH in VIPcre/tdTomato barrel cortex; layer-resolved morphology and intrinsic properties.

findings.claim: Immunocytochemistry showed that virtually all (95%) VIP-positive GABAergic terminals enriched in mGluR7a target mGluR1α/somatostatin-expressing interneurons (88% of targets), providing structural evidence that VIP→SST disinhibition is a hard-wired feature of cortical circuits.
findings.claim_source_sentence: On such dendrites virtually all (95%) vasoactive intestinal polypeptide (VIP) positive (GABAergic) terminals were enriched in mGluR7a.
findings.effect_size: 95% of VIP terminals mGluR7a+; 88% of their targets are mGluR1α/SST+
findings.evidence: Immuno-EM mapping of VIP/mGluR7a-positive terminal targets in cortical interneuron circuitry.

findings.claim: In rat dentate gyrus, three morphologically and neurochemically distinct VIP-immunoreactive interneuron types were identified: hilar interneuron-selective cells co-expressing calretinin (but not CCK) targeting non-principal neurons, VIP/CCK basket cells innervating granule-cell somata, and a third VIP/CR cell projecting to stratum moleculare — establishing that VIP-containing neurons are themselves heterogeneous, with VIP+CR cells preferentially targeting other interneurons and VIP+CCK cells targeting principal cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Three types of VIP-immunoreactive cells have been identified on the basis of these criteria: (i) cells forming a dense axonal plexus in the hilus have always coexisted with the calcium binding protein calretinin (CR), but never with the neuropeptide cholecystokinin (CCK).
findings.evidence: Immunocytochemistry + neuroanatomical analysis in rat dentate gyrus; defines three VIP cell types by marker co-expression and targeting.

findings.claim: Quantitative double-immunolabeling across mouse cortex established that parvalbumin, somatostatin, and VIP identify three reliably non-overlapping inhibitory subpopulations, while CR and NPY overlap substantially with SST (~21.6% and ~7.1% of SST+ cells respectively) — formalizing the canonical PV/SST/VIP partition in mouse but flagging marker promiscuity for CR.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We found that, PV, SOM, and vasointenstinal peptide (VIP) reliably identify three nonoverlapping distinct subpopulations, as there was no overlap of immunoreactivity between PV and all the other chemical markers tested, and SOM and VIP did not show any overlap in labeled neurons in all the cortical areas.
findings.evidence: Double immunochemistry across mouse cortical regions; quantification of marker overlap across PV, SST, VIP, CR, NPY, CCK.

findings.claim: Triple-immunostaining of mouse visual cortex resolved at least 13 distinct combinatorial groups of GABAergic neurons defined by overlapping expression of PV, CR, SOM, CCK, NPY, VIP, ChAT and additional markers, demonstrating that the cortical interneuron population is finer-grained than the canonical PV/SST/VIP/CR partition.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We have found at least 13 distinct groups of GABAergic neurons which include PV, CR, SOM, CCK (cholecystokinin), CR + SOM, CR + NPY (neuropeptide Y), CR + VIP (vasointestinal polypeptide), SOM + NPY, SOM + VIP, VIP + ChAT (choline acety
findings.evidence: Triple immunostaining for combinatorial marker groups in mouse visual cortex.

findings.claim: Whole-cell recording with biocytin labeling in rat frontal cortex defined parvalbumin fast-spiking basket/chandelier cells, late-spiking neurogliaform cells, and a heterogeneous regular- and burst-spiking group containing SST+ Martinotti cells (some calbindin+) and VIP+ double-bouquet/arcade cells (some CCK+ or CR+) — establishing the multi-marker, multi-modality framework that anchors all subsequent inhibitory taxonomies.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Several groups of GABAergic non-pyramidal cells were identified: (i) parvalbumin fast-spiking (FS) cells with low input resistances and spikes of short duration, including extended plexus (basket) cells and chandelier cells.
findings.evidence: Whole-cell patch-clamp + biocytin morphology + immunohistochemistry of rat frontal cortex non-pyramidal cells.
findings.study_system: rat frontal cortex slice patch + immunohistochemistry

findings.claim: In GAD67-GFP knock-in mice, GABAergic neurons constitute approximately 19.5% of NeuN+ frontal motor cortex neurons, with CR-, PV-, and SST-positive subsets all GFP+, providing the early quantitative scaffold for relative abundances of marker-defined cortical interneuron classes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: The proportion of GFP-positive cells among NeuN-positive cells (neocortical neurons) was approximately 19.5%.
findings.effect_size: GABAergic neurons ≈ 19.5% of frontal motor cortex neurons
findings.evidence: GAD67-GFP knock-in mouse: quantification of GABAergic neuron proportion and marker overlaps in motor cortex.

findings.claim: Light and electron microscopy in rat barrel cortex showed that essentially all calbindin-positive interneurons (214/222 with somatic appositions) receive symmetric VIP+ synaptic contacts on soma and dendrites, supporting a generalized VIP→CB-interneuron disinhibitory motif across all cortical layers.
findings.claim_source_sentence: A great majority of CB-immunoreactive interneurons (214/222) displayed such close appositions with VIP boutons on their soma as well.
findings.effect_size: 214/222 (96.4%) of CB+ interneurons receive VIP+ somatic appositions
findings.evidence: Double immuno + correlated light/EM in rat barrel cortex; quantification of VIP→CB+ synaptic contacts.
findings.study_system: rat barrel cortex light + EM immunocytochemistry

findings.claim: In rat cortex, all VIP-immunoreactive interneurons across cortical layers receive parvalbumin-positive basket-cell boutons (3 to >20 contacts per VIP cell), establishing that PV cells provide ubiquitous reciprocal inhibition onto VIP disinhibitory cells.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Abundant appositions between parvalbumin-immunoreactive boutons and all VIP-stained neurons were observed at the light microscopic level.
findings.effect_size: 3 to >20 PV+ contacts per VIP cell across all layers
findings.evidence: Correlated light/EM quantification of PV→VIP synaptic appositions in rat cortex.

findings.claim: Whole-cell recording in rat frontal cortex showed that cholinergic agonists depolarize VIP- and SST-immunoreactive interneurons but not PV-fast-spiking or late-spiking cells, while CCK cells respond heterogeneously by size, demonstrating that acetylcholine differentially gates marker-defined cortical interneuron subtypes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Carbachol (10 microM) or (+)-muscarine (3 microM) affected the activities of peptide-containing GABAergic cells with regular- or burst-spiking characteristics, but not of GABAergic cells with fast-spiking characteristics containing the calcium-binding protein parvalbumin or GABAergic cells with late-spiking characteristics.
findings.evidence: In vitro whole-cell recording + biocytin + immunohistochemistry; pharmacological cholinergic challenge of identified interneuron subtypes.
findings.study_system: rat frontal cortex slice + cholinergic agonists

findings.claim: Whole-cell recording with biocytin reconstruction in rat frontal cortex showed that SST+ and VIP+ non-pyramidal cells share a burst- or regular-spiking electrophysiology distinct from PV+ fast-spiking and late-spiking neurogliaform cells, with SST cells including layer-V Martinotti cells (some calbindin+) and VIP cells comprising bipolar, double-bouquet, and small basket morphologies — providing the foundational electrophysiological–morphological correspondence map for the four-group taxonomy.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We have found that most somatostatin- or VIP-containing neurons were different from two other types of GABAergic cells, the parvalbumin-containing fast-spiking cells and the late-spiking cells (neurogliaform cells).
findings.evidence: In vitro whole-cell recording + biocytin labeling + immunohistochemistry for SST/VIP in rat frontal cortex.
findings.study_system: rat frontal cortex slice patch + IHC

findings.claim: BAC-transgenic 5-HT3AR-GFP mice show that the ionotropic serotonin receptor 5-HT3AR is expressed in essentially all neocortical GABAergic interneurons that lack PV or SST expression, and genetic fate mapping shows that 5-HT3AR+ cells encompass the entire spectrum of CGE-derived interneurons — providing a single transgenic tool to access the third major (non-PV / non-SST) interneuron class.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Using a bacterial artificial chromosome transgenic mouse line, we show that, in the somatosensory cortex of the mouse, the serotonin 5-hydroxytryptamine 3A (5-HT(3A)) receptor, the only ionotropic serotonergic receptor, is expressed in most, if not all, neocortical GABAergic interneurons that do not express PV or SST.
findings.evidence: BAC transgenic 5-HT3AR-GFP mouse: characterization of fate-mapped CGE-derived interneurons; pharmacology of 5-HT and ACh responses.

findings.claim: Xu et al. (2013) found that, in mouse layer 4 somatosensory cortex, SST interneurons target fast-spiking PV cells rather than pyramidal cells, so optogenetic SST silencing decreases L4 principal-neuron firing — demonstrating an SST-mediated disinhibitory motif that breaks the canonical 'VIP-only' disinhibition narrative.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Accordingly, optogenetic inhibition of SOM INs in an active cortical network increases the firing of layers 2/3 PCs whereas it decreases the firing of layer 4 principal neurons (PNs).
findings.evidence: Optogenetic + slice-electrophysiology dissection of SST→FS→L4-PN microcircuit in mouse S1.
findings.study_system: mouse S1 layer 4 optogenetics + paired recordings

findings.claim: Dipoppa et al. (2018) imaged Pyr/SST/VIP/PV cells simultaneously in mouse V1: in darkness their data fit the canonical VIP→SST disinhibition model, but during visual stimulation the model failed — locomotion increased SST responses to large stimuli and VIP responses to small ones — implying that effective synaptic connectivity is modulated by behavioral state and visual context.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Our recordings in darkness were consistent with a "disinhibitory" model in which locomotion activates Vip cells, thus inhibiting Sst cells and disinhibiting Pyr cells.
findings.evidence: Simultaneous 2-photon Ca2+ imaging of four cell classes in mouse V1 across darkness and visual stimulus contexts; recurrent network modeling.
findings.study_system: mouse V1 in vivo 2-photon imaging + recurrent modeling

findings.claim: Long-range cingulate→V1 projections act through local GABAergic circuits to produce spatially structured top-down modulation: VIP interneurons are crucial for center facilitation while SST interneurons contribute preferentially to surround suppression — assigning distinct disinhibitory and inhibitory roles to VIP vs SST in cortical attention.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Whereas somatostatin-positive GABAergic interneurons contributed preferentially to surround suppression, vasoactive intestinal peptide-positive interneurons were crucial for center facilitation.
findings.evidence: Optogenetic activation of cingulate→V1 axons + cell-type-specific perturbation of VIP and SST interneurons; visual-discrimination behavior.

findings.claim: Intersectional Id2 genetics in mouse cortex reveals that, outside layer 1, most Id2-expressing interneurons are neurogliaform cells expressing high NPY with late-spiking physiology, while non-NGFC Id2 cells form a diverse, mostly CCK+ subgroup — supporting Id2 as a marker for a fourth major interneuron group complementing PV, SST, and VIP.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that outside of layer 1 (L1), the majority of Id2 INs are NGFCs that express high levels of neuropeptide Y (NPY) and exhibit a late-spiking firing pattern, with extensive local connectivity.
findings.evidence: Intersectional Id2 mouse genetics + slice patch + in vivo silicon-probe recordings characterizing NGFC and Id2-CCK subgroups.
findings.study_system: Id2 intersectional mouse cortex; in vivo silicon-probe + slice patch

findings.claim: Intersectional Flp/Cre crosses combined with retrograde dye labeling and IHC in mouse cortex identified two distinct layer-1-targeting SST subtypes with non-overlapping marker expression and electrophysiology, illustrating that even within the dendrite-targeting SST class — recently subdivided into 13 morpho-electric-transcriptomic (MET) types — meaningful subgroups can be accessed by combinatorial genetics.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We identified two layer 1-targeting subtypes with nonoverlapping marker protein expression and electrophysiological pr
findings.evidence: Flp/Cre intersectional reporter crosses + retrograde tracing + IHC + whole-cell recordings of SST cortical interneurons.
findings.study_system: mouse cortex SST intersectional genetics + slice patch

findings.claim: Ma et al. (2006) showed that three transgenic mouse lines (X94, X98, GIN) labeling distinct subsets of cortical somatostatin interneurons displayed nearly perfect segregation of laminar distribution, neurochemistry, axonal targets and intrinsic electrophysiology — providing one of the earliest demonstrations that multiple converging classification criteria identify the same SST subtypes.
findings.claim_source_sentence: By all criteria, there was nearly perfect segregation of X94 and X98 GFP+ neurons, whereas GIN GFP+ neurons exhibited intermediate properties.
findings.evidence: GAD67-promoter GFP transgenic mouse lines X94, X98, GIN; whole-cell patch + IHC of 150 SOM+ neurons.
findings.study_system: mouse barrel cortex SOM transgenic lines + slice patch

findings.claim: Urban-Ciecko & Barth (2016) review evidence that cortical somatostatin interneurons comprise multiple subpopulations distinguishable by morphology, connectivity, intralaminar inputs, firing properties, and markers, and that distinct SST subclasses participate in distinct disinhibitory circuits with different inhibitory partners and cortical layers — arguing against treating SST as a homogenous functional class.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Different classes of SOM cells participate in distinct disinhibitory circuits with different inhibitory partners and in different cortical layers.
findings.evidence: Review of SOM interneuron heterogeneity in sensory cortex.

findings.claim: Whole-cell patch + RT-PCR of 180 Martinotti cells in juvenile rat S1 demonstrated that Martinotti cells in different layers target distinct dendritic compartments (e.g., layer II/III MCs target mainly layer 1, layer IV MCs target mainly layer 4) and exhibit predominantly accommodating but also non-accommodating and bursting electrophysiology — heterogeneity within a single morphologically defined SST subtype.
findings.claim_source_sentence: MCs were found to contact tuft, apical and basal dendrites in multiple neocortical layers: layer II/III MCs targeted mostly layer I and to a lesser degree layer II/III; layer IV MCs targeted mostly layer IV and to a lesser degree layer I; layer V and VI MCs targeted mostly layer IV and layer I and to a lesser degree the layer in which their somata was located.
findings.evidence: Whole-cell patch-clamp + biocytin morphology + single-cell multiplex RT-PCR of 180 Martinotti interneurons in juvenile rat S1.
findings.study_system: rat S1 cortex slice patch + scRT-PCR

findings.claim: A review of parvalbumin interneuron biology emphasizes that PV+ cells are the only cortical interneuron subclass with myelinated axons, fast-spiking ability that outpaces other cortical neurons, dendritic linearity and inhibitory autapses — biophysical specializations dedicated to driving cortical gamma oscillations.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Myelinated axon (which is only found in the PV+ subclass of interneurons) to secure fast-spiking at the initial axon segment
findings.evidence: Review of structural and biophysical specializations of parvalbumin cortical interneurons.

findings.claim: Consensus non-negative matrix factorization (cNMF) applied to brain-organoid and visual-cortex scRNA-seq decomposes each cell's expression into identity and activity programs (e.g., cell cycle, hypoxia), refining cell-type calls — methodological evidence that 'cell type' from scRNA-seq mixes type-defining and transient activity programs.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show with simulations that a method we call consensus non-negative matrix factorization (cNMF) accurately infers identity and activity programs, including their relative contributions in each cell.
findings.evidence: Method paper: matrix factorization (cNMF) for identity vs activity decomposition; benchmarked on simulations and applied to brain-organoid + V1 scRNA-seq.

findings.claim: Integrating 43 scRNA-seq datasets with GWAS summary statistics for 26 traits identified independent cell-type associations and provided the FUMA platform — establishing scRNA-seq cell-type catalogues as a vehicle for assigning genetic risk to specific neuronal types.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Applying this method to 26 traits, we identify independent associations of multiple cell types.
findings.evidence: Curation of 43 public scRNA-seq datasets integrated with GWAS summary stats for 26 traits via FUMA-MAGMA workflow.

findings.claim: Multi-modal single-cell transcriptomic + ATAC + Stereo-seq spatial profiling of 358,237 cells from cynomolgus PFC, M1 and V1 mapped area-specific chromatin-accessible regulatory elements onto cell types and identified excitatory-neuron-specific transcriptional regulatory networks underlying area specialization.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We generated single-cell transcriptomic and chromatin accessibility (single-cell ATAC) data of 358,237 cells from prefrontal cortex (PFC), primary motor cortex (M1) and primary visual cortex (V1) of adult female cynomolgus monkey brain, and integrated this dataset with Stereo-seq (spatial enhanced resolution omics-sequencing) of the corresponding cortical areas to assign topographic information to molecular states.
findings.evidence: Multi-modal sc transcriptomic + scATAC + Stereo-seq across three cynomolgus cortical areas (n=358,237 cells).
findings.study_system: cynomolgus monkey PFC/M1/V1 sc multi-omics + spatial

findings.claim: Single-cell multiplex RT-PCR of CB/PV/CR/NPY/VIP/SST/CCK in 268 morphologically identified rat S1 interneurons revealed seven distinct expression-based clusters that preferentially contained one anatomical class — early evidence that combinatorial neuropeptide/calcium-binding-protein codes predict morphological subtype.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Quality threshold clustering, an unsupervised algorithm that clustered neurones according to their entire profile of expressed genes, revealed seven distinct clusters.
findings.evidence: Patch-clamp + biocytin morphology + single-cell multiplex RT-PCR for 7 markers in 268 juvenile rat S1 interneurons.
findings.study_system: rat S1 cortex single-cell multiplex RT-PCR + biocytin

findings.claim: Two-photon optogenetic activation of single VIP interneurons in mouse V1 in vivo combined with calcium imaging showed that VIP cells have narrow axons that inhibit nearby SST interneurons, which then disinhibit pyramidal cells — supporting a focal, 'spotlight-of-attention' lateral disinhibition mechanism rather than spatially diffuse VIP signaling.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that VIP interneurons have narrow axons and inhibit nearby somatostatin interneurons, which themselves inhibit pyramidal cells.
findings.evidence: Two-photon single-cell optogenetic activation + 2-photon Ca2+ imaging of VIP→SST→Pyr microcircuit in mouse V1 in vivo.
findings.study_system: mouse V1 in vivo 2-photon optogenetics + imaging

findings.claim: In vivo two-photon imaging in mouse V1 showed that basal forebrain cholinergic activation differentially modulates cortical interneuron classes — VIP and layer 1 interneurons are preferentially activated through nicotinic receptors during strong desynchronization, while excitatory and PV+ neurons engage muscarinic and nicotinic receptors with opposite signs at different arousal levels — substantiating cholinergic neuromodulation as a switch driving the canonical VIP disinhibitory motif.
findings.claim_source_sentence: In contrast, vasoactive intestinal peptide-positive (VIP+) and layer 1 interneurons were preferentially activated through nAChRs during strong cortical desynchronization.
findings.evidence: In vivo 2-photon Ca2+ imaging of L1/L2-3 mouse V1 + electrical/pharmacological basal-forebrain stimulation.
findings.study_system: mouse V1 in vivo 2-photon Ca2+ imaging + basal forebrain manipulation

findings.claim: Systematic evaluation of six cell-type-matching algorithms across four image-based spatial transcriptomics protocols (MERFISH, smFISH, BaristaSeq, ExSeq) in mouse VISp showed that many cells receive consistent type assignments across methods and that ensemble consensus increases alignment with prior scRNA-seq-defined VISp cell types.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We find that many cells are assigned as the same type by multiple cell type matching algorithms and are present in spatial patterns previously reported from scRNA-seq studies in VISp.
findings.evidence: Benchmarking 6 cell-type-matching algorithms × 4 image-based spatial transcriptomics protocols on the same mouse VISp dataset.
findings.study_system: mouse V1 (VISp) image-based spatial transcriptomics benchmark

findings.claim: Upper-layer (L1-III) and deeper-layer (L4-VI) cortical VIP+ interneurons are transcriptionally distinct, with Tac2 and Cxcl14 emerging as conserved upper-layer marker genes across mouse VISp, ALM, MOp and in human cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Cross-comparing between the top 10 differentially expressed genes (DEGs) with Allen Mouse Brain in situ hybridization database, we identified Tac2 and CxCl14 as potential marker genes of upper layer VIP+ interneurons across most cortical regions. Importantly, such expression patterns are conserved in the human brain.
findings.evidence: scRNA-seq analysis of 1175 high-quality VIP+ interneurons in mouse VISp from public databases, replicated in ALM and MOp; cross-validated with Allen ISH; conservation confirmed in human cortex transcriptome.
findings.study_system: mouse and human cortex; scRNA-seq + Allen ISH

findings.claim: L5 SST interneurons receive intralaminar inhibition from both PV and VIP cells with cell-type-specific short-term plasticity (PV→SST depressing; VIP→SST facilitating at high frequency), and translaminar input from L2/3 VIP—but not L2/3 PV—cells, reproducing the disinhibitory motif previously characterized in supragranular layers.
findings.claim_source_sentence: PV to SST connections were depressing at all tested frequencies, whereas VIP to SST connections were facilitating at high-frequency VIP IN stimulation. In addition translaminar inputs from L2/3 VIP to L5 SST IN showed similar connectivity rates and short-term plasticity compared to their L5 counterparts. However L2/3 PV IN, despite numerous descending axon collaterals, showed hardly any connection.
findings.evidence: Triple transgenic mice; paired whole-cell patch-clamp recordings in acute somatosensory cortex slices; intra- and translaminar connection rates and short-term plasticity assessed.
findings.study_system: mouse somatosensory cortex L5; paired patch-clamp

findings.claim: NECAB1 and NECAB2 (N-terminal EF-hand calcium-binding proteins) are predominant calcium-binding proteins of CB1/CCK-positive GABAergic interneurons—closing a long-standing gap in which this large interneuron class lacked typifying CaBPs (in contrast to PV+, calbindin+, calretinin+ classes).
findings.claim_source_sentence: By in silico data mining in mouse single-cell RNA-seq databases, we identified high expression of Necab1 and Necab2 genes encoding N-terminal EF-hand calcium-binding proteins 1 and 2, respectively, in CB1/CCK-positive interneurons. ... These findings highlight NECAB1 and NECAB2 as predominant calcium-binding proteins in CB1/CCK-positive interneurons.
findings.evidence: In silico scRNA-seq data mining + fluorescent in situ hybridization + immunostaining + patch-clamp + STORM super-resolution microscopy across mouse isocortex, hippocampus, and basolateral amygdala.
findings.study_system: mouse isocortex, hippocampus, BLA; multimodal (scRNA + FISH + IHC + STORM)

findings.claim: Across mouse, human, songbird, and turtle forebrains, the first principal component of the GABAergic interneuron gene-expression matrix—shown in mouse to predict state modulation—diverges between species due both to within-cell-type expression divergence and to cell-type abundance divergence; cholinergic receptors predictive of state modulation in mouse are differentially expressed across species, indicating evolutionary divergence of state-modulation gene programs despite cell-type homology.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Despite homology at the level of cell types, we found clear differences between transcriptomic PCs, with greater dissimilarities between evolutionarily distant species. These dissimilarities arise from two factors: divergence in gene expression within homologous cell types and divergence in cell-type abundance. ... Several cholinergic receptors predictive of state modulation in mouse interneurons are differentially expressed between species.
findings.evidence: Meta-analysis of seven scRNA/snRNA-seq datasets from mouse, human, songbird, and turtle forebrains; PC analysis of gene expression; cholinergic receptor comparison; circuit modelling.
findings.study_system: mouse, human, songbird, turtle forebrains; cross-species scRNA/snRNA-seq meta-analysis

findings.claim: snRNA-seq + spatial transcriptomic comparison of V1 in marsupial Monodelphis domestica vs eutherian Mus musculus reveals that supragranular vs infragranular IT-neuron transcriptomic distinctions are more pronounced in mouse, and mouse cortex has lower PV+ GABAergic density and redistributed perineuronal nets — challenging uniform conservation of cortical column architecture across deeply diverged mammals.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We show that spatio-transcriptomic distinctions between supragranular (layer 2/3) and infragranular (layer 5) intratelencephalic neurons are more pronounced in mice, consistent with lineage-specific specialization. Mouse cortex also exhibits a lower relative density of parvalbumin-positive GABAergic neurons and redistributed perineuronal nets, consistent with altered constraints on plasticity.
findings.evidence: snRNA-seq and spatial transcriptomics on V1 of opossum (M. domestica) vs mouse (M. musculus); cross-species cell-type, lamination, and PV/PNN comparison.
findings.study_system: Monodelphis domestica + Mus musculus V1; snRNA-seq + spatial transcriptomics

findings.claim: VIP/calretinin interneuron-selective cells implement a conserved disinhibitory motif across cortical areas: they specialize in inhibiting other GABAergic interneurons, and their activation enhances learning, memory, and sensory discrimination performance — supporting the proposal that interneuron-targeting interneurons constitute a fundamental cortical microcircuit module.
findings.claim_source_sentence: We make the case that, across cortical structures, interneuron-specific cells commonly specialize in the synaptic disinhibition of excitatory neurons, which can ungate the integration and plasticity of external inputs onto excitatory neurons. In line with this, activation of interneuron- specific cells enhances animal performance across a variety of behavioral tasks that involve learning, memory formation, and sensory discrimination.
findings.evidence: Review synthesis of in vivo and in vitro studies of VIP/CR-expressing cells across multiple cortical areas (sensory, motor, prefrontal, hippocampus).

findings.claim: A subpopulation of cortical interneurons—predominantly somatostatin-expressing and located in layer 5—exhibits precocious GABAergic functional maturation: KCC2 expression at embryonic stages, hyperpolarizing GABA_A responses at birth, and elevated synaptic activity, in contrast to the canonical postnatal-week-1 depolarizing-to-hyperpolarizing GABA shift in principal neurons; these early-KCC2 interneurons are evolutionarily conserved in human cortex.
findings.claim_source_sentence: Spatial transcriptomics and differential gene expression (DGE) analyses reveal early KCC2-expressing INs localize predominantly to layer 5, express somatostatin, and show upregulation of synaptogenic genes, consistent with the recorded elevated synaptic activity. Crucially, evolutionary conservation of early KCC2-expressing INs in humans was demonstrated with analogous genetic profiles enriched for signaling and synaptic maturation pathways.
findings.evidence: High-resolution mouse cortical development atlas + scRNA-seq + spatial transcriptomics + electrophysiology; cross-species comparison with human snRNA-seq.
findings.study_system: mouse + human cortex; multimodal scRNA-seq, spatial transcriptomics, and electrophysiology
